compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The enriched sidecars (stories 4e19/7c84/e7e0) and the existing attestations are reachable today
only via scattered internal helpers; there is no single call that returns a ticket's FULL retained
review history or resolves its associated code reviews. The follow-on audit UI epic and any script
need one documented read surface.

## What

A read layer that, for a ticket id, returns: (a) the full retained history (not just latest) of
plan-review sidecars; (b) the completion attestation plus any completion sidecar record; (c) the
associated code reviews, resolved via the ticket's inbound `relates_to` links from `code_review`
tickets, each with its full sidecar history. Exposed as a library function, a CLI command, and a
read-only MCP tool.

## Data contract (the returned typed dict)

`audit_trail(ticket_id)` returns a `TypedDict` with EXACT key names and nullability:

```python
class CompletionRecord(TypedDict):
    attestation: dict | None   # the ticket's completion-verifier attestation (from show/keyring); None if unsigned
    sidecar: dict | None       # the latest completion sidecar record — a PASS (completion_verifier_pass_v1)
                               # or FAIL (completion_verifier_fail_v1) record; None if no sidecar event

class AuditTrail(TypedDict):
    ticket: dict                    # the ticket's current details (from rebar.show)
    plan_reviews: list[dict]        # full retained plan-review sidecar history, newest-first; [] if none
    completion: CompletionRecord | None  # None ONLY when BOTH attestation AND sidecar are absent
    code_reviews: list[dict]        # each: {ticket_id, sidecars: list[dict]} — a linked code_review
                                    # ticket + its own newest-first sidecar history; [] if none
```

- `plan_reviews` is ALWAYS a list (empty `[]` when the ticket has no plan-review sidecars), newest first.
- `completion` is a `CompletionRecord` dict when EITHER a completion-verifier attestation OR a
  completion sidecar record exists; it is `None` ONLY when BOTH are absent. The two sub-fields come
  from two DISJOINT sources (the attestation lives on the ticket; the sidecar is a `COMPLETION_VERDICT`
  event), so each is independently nullable inside the record.
- `code_reviews` is ALWAYS a list (empty `[]` when no `code_review` ticket links to this ticket via
  `relates_to`); each entry carries its OWN sidecar history list.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` and `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — add
  `all_review_results(ticket)` history enumerators (walk the ticket's `REVIEW_RESULT` events
  newest→oldest, schema-tolerant, returning the list of payload dicts; `[]` on missing/error),
  complementing the existing `latest_*` readers.
- `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` — the completion sidecar reader for the `completion.sidecar`
  field: reuse `latest_pass_record` (PASS) and `latest_fail_verdict` (FAIL) — the aggregator returns
  whichever is newest/present (no new reader needed unless a "latest of either kind" helper is
  cleaner; if added, keep it schema-tolerant over both `completion_verifier_pass_v1` and `_fail_v1`).
- New `src/rebar/audit/read.py` — an `audit_trail(ticket_id, *, repo_root=None)` aggregator composing
  the plan / completion / code-review histories, resolving code reviews via the ticket's inbound
  `relates_to` links from `code_review` tickets (reusing `rebar.show` / `rebar.list_tickets`),
  returning the `AuditTrail` typed dict above. Also add `__init__.py` for the new `rebar.audit`
  package.
- CLI registration (owns-its-own-`--help` intercept pattern used by `_workflow`/`_llm`/`_criteria`):
  - add an `audit` intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py::main`, dispatching to an
    `audit_cli(rest)` handler in a new `src/rebar/_cli/_audit_commands.py` (parses
    `show <ticket> [--output json]` and prints the `AuditTrail`).
  - **create `src/rebar/_cli/help/audit.txt`** (the usage text for `rebar audit show`). This is what
    makes `audit` a recognized subcommand: `known_subcommands()` is NOT a hardcoded set — it is
    DERIVED from the `.txt` files under `src/rebar/_cli/help/` (every `<name>.txt` except
    `overview.txt`). So adding the help file registers the subcommand; do NOT edit `known_subcommands`.
- MCP read-tool registration: add a read-only `audit_trail(ticket_id)` tool inside
  `src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py::register_read_tools` (decorated with `@mcp.tool(annotations=_ANN
  ["READ_ONLY"])`, like the other read tools), so it is available even under `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1`.

## Approach rationale

A single aggregator over the existing readers + the existing `relates_to` links reuses the linkage
the reviewbot already writes (`emit_code_review_artifact` links each code-review artifact
`relates_to` its work ticket), so no new linking mechanism is introduced — the read layer only
traverses links that already exist. The MCP tool is read-only so it is available regardless of
`REBAR_MCP_READONLY`.

## Compatibility & rollback

Pure addition (new readers + new command + new read-only tool); no existing behavior changes.
Schema-tolerant readers accept both v1 and v2 records. Reverting removes the new surface (delete
`rebar.audit`, `_audit_commands.py`, `help/audit.txt`, the intercept, the MCP tool, the
`all_review_results` readers) with no data impact.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `audit_trail(ticket_id)` returns a dict with EXACTLY the keys `ticket`, `plan_reviews`,
      `completion`, `code_reviews`; `plan_reviews` and `code_reviews` are lists (newest-first,
      `[]` when none), `completion` is `CompletionRecord | None` (`None` only when both attestation
      and sidecar are absent). Oracle: an integration test seeding a ticket with multiple plan-review
      sidecars, a completion record, and a `relates_to`-linked `code_review` ticket with its own
      sidecar, then asserting the exact key set, the newest-first ordering of `plan_reviews`, that
      `completion` is a dict, and that `code_reviews[0]` carries its own sidecar history; plus a
      companion assertion that a bare ticket yields `plan_reviews == []`, `completion is None`,
      `code_reviews == []`.
- [ ] `rebar audit show <ticket> --output json` prints the same structure. Oracle: a CLI subprocess
      test asserting on the emitted JSON's key set.
- [ ] The MCP `audit_trail` tool returns the structure and is read-only (available even with
      `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1`). Oracle: a test invoking the tool handler under that env.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

file_impact.reason: completion pass/fail readers composed into trail

compiled_state.title: audit read layer (full-history readers + relates_to resolution + CLI/MCP)
